questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding the National Crisis Management Committee (NCMC): 1. It has been granted statutory status under the Disaster Management Act. 2. It is headed by the Union Home Minister. 3. It acts as the nodal body to lay down policies for Disaster Management. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The NCMC was granted statutory status under the Disaster Management Act (Amendment) Act, 2025. Statement 2 is incorrect: It is headed by the Cabinet Secretary, not the Union Home Minister. Statement 3 is correct: NCMC acts as the nodal body to deal with major disasters, evaluate preparedness, and lay down policies for Disaster Management.
questions.takeaway: The NCMC is a statutory body headed by the Cabinet Secretary that functions as the nodal agency for handling major disasters and laying down disaster management policies.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: NCMC was granted statutory status under the Disaster Management Act (Amendment) Act, 2025. It is headed by the Cabinet Secretary. NCMC acts as the nodal body to deal with major disasters, evaluate preparedness, and lay down policies for Disaster Management.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: Consider the following statements regarding the National Crisis Management Committee (NCMC): 1. The procedural rules governing the NCMC are notified by the Cabinet Secretariat. 2. It evaluates preparedness and acts as the nodal body to deal with major disasters. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Ministry of Home Affairs notified the Disaster Management NCMC (Procedure) Rules, 2025, not the Cabinet Secretariat. Statement 2 is correct: The NCMC acts as the nodal body to deal with major disasters and evaluate preparedness.
questions.takeaway: While the NCMC is headed by the Cabinet Secretary, its procedural rules under the amended Disaster Management Act are notified by the Ministry of Home Affairs.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Ministry of Home Affairs Notified Disaster Management NCMC (Procedure) Rules, 2025. ... NCMC acts as the nodal body to deal with major disasters, evaluate preparedness, etc.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: Consider the following pairs regarding the institutional framework of the National Crisis Management Committee (NCMC): 1. Legal Status : Statutory body under the Disaster Management Act 2. Chairperson : Cabinet Secretary 3. Notifying authority for procedural rules : Ministry of Home Affairs. How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched: The NCMC was granted statutory status under the Disaster Management Act (Amendment) Act, 2025. Pair 2 is correctly matched: The committee is headed by the Cabinet Secretary. Pair 3 is correctly matched: The Ministry of Home Affairs is the authority that notified the Disaster Management NCMC (Procedure) Rules, 2025.
questions.takeaway: The NCMC recently transitioned to a statutory body under the DM Act, with its procedural rules governed by the MHA and leadership falling under the Cabinet Secretary.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Ministry of Home Affairs Notified Disaster Management NCMC (Procedure) Rules, 2025. Background: Disaster Management Act (Amendment) Act, 2025, granted statutory status to NCMC. Composition: Headed by Cabinet Secretary
